task: I need to send a client a quick update because a deliverable is running later than planned, and I want the message to be clear and professional. Can you help me word it so it explains the situation briefly and says what I’m doing next?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a clean, professional version you can send:

**Subject:** Quick update on [Deliverable Name]

Hi [Client Name],  
I wanted to give you a quick update that [deliverable] is running a bit later than planned. I’m working through [brief reason, if appropriate], and my next step is [what you’re doing next].  

I expect to have it to you by [new time/date]. I’ll keep you posted if anything changes.  

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make this:
- more formal
- more casual
- shorter/text-message style

user_simulator.text:
Good enough as a base, but it’s a little generic. I’d want it tighter and less apologetic. Something like:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: [deliverable] is taking longer than planned because [brief reason]. I’m working on [next step] now and expect to send it by [new time/date].

I’ll let you know if anything changes.

Best,
[Your Name]

If you can, give me a version that sounds a bit more direct and a bit more polished for a client who expects concise updates.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, more direct version that still sounds polished:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: [deliverable] is taking longer than planned due to [brief reason]. I’m on [next step] now and expect to send it by [new time/date].

I’ll keep you posted if anything changes.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also give you:
- a slightly firmer version
- a more polished/client-facing version
- a version with no mention of the reason at all

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Tight enough, and not too chatty. I can use that.
